channel.title: Recent Articles in Phys. Rev. Lett.
channel.description: Recent articles in Physical Review Letters
channel.dc:creator: rss@aps.org
channel.dc:rights: Copyright © 2025 the American Physical Society. Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Circular Dichroism on the Edge of Quantum Hall Systems: From Many-Body Chern Number to Anisotropy Measurements
item.description:
Author(s): F. Nur Ünal, A. Nardin, and N. Goldman

Quantum Hall states are characterized by a topological invariant, the many-body Chern number, which determines their quantized Hall conductivity. This invariant also emerges in circular dichroic responses, namely, by applying a circular drive and comparing excitation rates for opposite orientations.…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 266603] Published Wed Dec 24, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): F. Nur Ünal, A. Nardin, and N. Goldman

Quantum Hall states are characterized by a topological invariant, the many-body Chern number, which determines their quantized Hall conductivity. This invariant also emerges in circular dichroic responses, namely, by applying a circular drive and comparing excitation rates for opposite orientations.…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 266603] Published Wed Dec 24, 2025
item.dc:title: Circular Dichroism on the Edge of Quantum Hall Systems: From Many-Body Chern Number to Anisotropy Measurements
item.dc:creator: F. Nur Ünal, A. Nardin, and N. Goldman
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Fast Hydrogen Atom Diffraction through Monocrystalline Graphene
item.description:
Author(s): Pierre Guichard, Arnaud Dochain, Raphaël Marion, Pauline de Crombrugghe de Picquendaele, Nicolas Lejeune, Benoît Hackens, Paul-Antoine Hervieux, and Xavier Urbain

A fast beam of hydrogen atoms passes through a graphene layer and produces a diffraction pattern, a development that could lead to a new probing technique of surface interactions.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 263403] Published Tue Dec 23, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Pierre Guichard, Arnaud Dochain, Raphaël Marion, Pauline de Crombrugghe de Picquendaele, Nicolas Lejeune, Benoît Hackens, Paul-Antoine Hervieux, and Xavier Urbain

A fast beam of hydrogen atoms passes through a graphene layer and produces a diffraction pattern, a development that could lead to a new probing technique of surface interactions.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 263403] Published Tue Dec 23, 2025
item.dc:title: Fast Hydrogen Atom Diffraction through Monocrystalline Graphene
item.dc:creator: Pierre Guichard, Arnaud Dochain, Raphaël Marion, Pauline de Crombrugghe de Picquendaele, Nicolas Lejeune, Benoît Hackens, Paul-Antoine Hervieux, and Xavier Urbain
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Atomic, Molecular, and Optical Physics

item.title: Spin-Depairing-Induced Exceptional Fermionic Superfluidity
item.description:
Author(s): Soma Takemori, Kazuki Yamamoto, and Akihisa Koga

We investigate the non-Hermitian (NH) attractive Hubbard model with spin depairing, which is a spin-resolved asymmetric hopping that nonreciprocally operates spins in the opposite direction. We find that spin depairing stabilizes a superfluid state unique to the NH system. This phase is characterize…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 266002] Published Tue Dec 23, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Soma Takemori, Kazuki Yamamoto, and Akihisa Koga

We investigate the non-Hermitian (NH) attractive Hubbard model with spin depairing, which is a spin-resolved asymmetric hopping that nonreciprocally operates spins in the opposite direction. We find that spin depairing stabilizes a superfluid state unique to the NH system. This phase is characterize…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 266002] Published Tue Dec 23, 2025
item.dc:title: Spin-Depairing-Induced Exceptional Fermionic Superfluidity
item.dc:creator: Soma Takemori, Kazuki Yamamoto, and Akihisa Koga
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Gate-Tunable Spectrum and Charge Dispersion Mitigation in a Graphene Superconducting Qubit
item.description:
Author(s): Nicolas Aparicio, Simon Messelot, Edgar Bonet-Orozco, Eric Eyraud, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Johann Coraux, and Julien Renard

Controlling the energy spectrum of quantum-coherent superconducting circuits, i.e., the energies of excited states, the circuit anharmonicity, and the states’ charge dispersion, is essential for designing performant qubits. This control is usually achieved by adjusting the circuit’s geometry. In sit…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 266001] Published Mon Dec 22, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Nicolas Aparicio, Simon Messelot, Edgar Bonet-Orozco, Eric Eyraud, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Johann Coraux, and Julien Renard

Controlling the energy spectrum of quantum-coherent superconducting circuits, i.e., the energies of excited states, the circuit anharmonicity, and the states’ charge dispersion, is essential for designing performant qubits. This control is usually achieved by adjusting the circuit’s geometry. In sit…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 266001] Published Mon Dec 22, 2025
item.dc:title: Gate-Tunable Spectrum and Charge Dispersion Mitigation in a Graphene Superconducting Qubit
item.dc:creator: Nicolas Aparicio, Simon Messelot, Edgar Bonet-Orozco, Eric Eyraud, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Johann Coraux, and Julien Renard

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Quantum Geometry in Phonon-Mediated Optical Responses
item.description:
Author(s): Jiaming Hu, Wenbin Li, Zhichao Guo, Hua Wang, and Kai Chang

Quantum geometry is crucial for understanding intricate condensed matter systems, governing transport phenomena and optical responses. However, traditional studies of quantum geometry predominantly consider a static crystal lattice, focusing exclusively on the pure-electronic quantum geometry of the…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256404] Published Thu Dec 18, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jiaming Hu, Wenbin Li, Zhichao Guo, Hua Wang, and Kai Chang

Quantum geometry is crucial for understanding intricate condensed matter systems, governing transport phenomena and optical responses. However, traditional studies of quantum geometry predominantly consider a static crystal lattice, focusing exclusively on the pure-electronic quantum geometry of the…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256404] Published Thu Dec 18, 2025
item.dc:title: Quantum Geometry in Phonon-Mediated Optical Responses
item.dc:creator: Jiaming Hu, Wenbin Li, Zhichao Guo, Hua Wang, and Kai Chang

item.title: Quantum Geometric Origin of Strain-Induced Ferroelectric Phase Transitions
item.description:
Author(s): Jiaming Hu, Ziye Zhu, Yubo Yuan, Hua Wang, and Kai Chang

Strain-regulated ferroelectric (FE) materials have long attracted significant attention due to their diverse applications. While soft-phonon theory and the (pseudo) Jahn-Teller effect have achieved considerable success in providing phenomenological descriptions and general understanding, the detaile…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256405] Published Thu Dec 18, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jiaming Hu, Ziye Zhu, Yubo Yuan, Hua Wang, and Kai Chang

Strain-regulated ferroelectric (FE) materials have long attracted significant attention due to their diverse applications. While soft-phonon theory and the (pseudo) Jahn-Teller effect have achieved considerable success in providing phenomenological descriptions and general understanding, the detaile…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256405] Published Thu Dec 18, 2025
item.dc:title: Quantum Geometric Origin of Strain-Induced Ferroelectric Phase Transitions
item.dc:creator: Jiaming Hu, Ziye Zhu, Yubo Yuan, Hua Wang, and Kai Chang

item.title: Chiral Wigner Crystal Phases Induced by Berry Curvature
item.description:
Author(s): Sandeep Joy, Leonid Levitov, and Brian Skinner

We study how Berry phase affects the Wigner crystal (WC) state of a two-dimensional electron system. We consider first a model of Bernal bilayer graphene with a perpendicular displacement field, and we show that Berry curvature leads to a new kind of WC state in which the electrons acquire a spontan…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256502] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Sandeep Joy, Leonid Levitov, and Brian Skinner

We study how Berry phase affects the Wigner crystal (WC) state of a two-dimensional electron system. We consider first a model of Bernal bilayer graphene with a perpendicular displacement field, and we show that Berry curvature leads to a new kind of WC state in which the electrons acquire a spontan…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256502] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.dc:title: Chiral Wigner Crystal Phases Induced by Berry Curvature
item.dc:creator: Sandeep Joy, Leonid Levitov, and Brian Skinner

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Fractional Topological States in Rhombohedral Multilayer Graphene Modulated by Kagome Superlattice
item.description:
Author(s): Yanran Shi, Bo Xie, Fengfan Ren, Xinyu Cai, Zhongqing Guo, Qiao Li, Xin Lu, Nicolas Regnault, Zhongkai Liu, and Jianpeng Liu

Rhombohedral multilayer graphene coupled with a patterned kagome superlattice produces various zero-magnetic-field fractional topological states.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256603] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yanran Shi, Bo Xie, Fengfan Ren, Xinyu Cai, Zhongqing Guo, Qiao Li, Xin Lu, Nicolas Regnault, Zhongkai Liu, and Jianpeng Liu

Rhombohedral multilayer graphene coupled with a patterned kagome superlattice produces various zero-magnetic-field fractional topological states.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 135, 256603] Published Wed Dec 17, 2025
item.dc:title: Fractional Topological States in Rhombohedral Multilayer Graphene Modulated by Kagome Superlattice
item.dc:creator: Yanran Shi, Bo Xie, Fengfan Ren, Xinyu Cai, Zhongqing Guo, Qiao Li, Xin Lu, Nicolas Regnault, Zhongkai Liu, and Jianpeng Liu
